data.title: Jakob's Law of Internet User Experience
data.description: Users spend most of their time on other sites, so they prefer products that work similarly to those they already know.
data.technical_spec: Emphasis on common design patterns and navigation structures.

data.title: Fitts’s Law
data.description: The time required to move to a target depends on the distance to and size of the target.
data.technical_spec: Large interactive elements, crucial for tools and buttons.

data.title: Hick’s Law
data.description: The more options users are presented with, the longer it will take them to make a decision.
data.technical_spec: Optimized menu structure with clear categorization.

data.title: Aesthetic-Usability Effect
data.description: Users perceive aesthetically pleasing designs as more usable, influencing their interactions.
data.technical_spec: Adobe's strong emphasis on UI/UX aesthetics and functional design.

data.title: Tesler’s Law (Law of Conservation of Complexity)
data.description: Every application has an inherent amount of irreducible complexity; smart designs aim to offload complexity from users.
data.technical_spec: Simplified task flows with automated suggestions.
